name: Pick-and-transport for e-commerce
summary: AMRs carry totes or accompany associates so walking time drops on multi-zone picks. Works best when Wi-Fi and a basic WMS handshake exist and aisles stay wide enough for collaborative traffic.

name: Fixed-lane carton and pallet moves
summary: AGVs suit stable point-to-point routes (dock to buffer, buffer to pack) when the map rarely changes. Infrastructure and guide paths raise upfront cost but stabilize repetitive volume.

name: Collaborative pick assist
summary: Pick-assist fleets bring work to associates or guide picks in zone. Highest payoff when pick density is high and WMS/WES can allocate work to robots and people together.

name: After-hours office vacuuming
summary: Compact robots cover open carpet and hard floors overnight while staff handle restrooms and detailing. Mapping and obstacle handling matter more than industrial scrub depth.

name: Food running in full-service dining
summary: Serving robots reduce runner walk time on clear paths during peak. Staff still plate, sell, and handle exceptions; robots are a capacity layer, not a full front-of-house replacement.
